mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days specifically during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 2.5% annually, or the signal predictive power vanishes outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5% annualized confirms robust economic expansion, boosting corporate profit expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are negative in > 60% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.5%, or the signal shows no predictive edge after controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index signal a tightening monetary policy response to inflation, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint rise in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fails to predict negative 60-day returns, or the interaction effect is dominated by single-series effects in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200,000 signal strong labor demand and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during low-volatility market regimes.
failure_condition: PAYEMS surges above 200,000 coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases, or the signal alpha decays to zero when market volatility spikes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in housing starts reflects expanding residential construction activity and consumer confidence, consistently driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Rank IC for HOUST momentum falls below 0.01, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with housing starts trends.
